feat: parameterize encoder for variable resolution, verified at 720p

All RTL modules now derive widths/sizes from FRAME_WIDTH/FRAME_HEIGHT
parameters. SPS generates resolution fields dynamically using UE(v)
Exp-Golomb encoding (following openh264 WelsWriteSpsSyntax). Tested
and verified with ffmpeg decode at both 320x176 and 1280x720.

- h264_encoder_top: parameterized module, widened addr/counter regs
- h264_bitstream: dynamic SPS with UE-encoded resolution, auto level_idc
- h264_me: widened coordinates/addresses for larger frames
- h264_fetch: parameterized FRAME_WIDTH, widened addresses
- Makefile: WIDTH/HEIGHT vars passed via -G (Verilog) and -D (C++)
- Testbench: compile-time FRAME_W/FRAME_H defines

commit.message:
Fix etcd test flakiness with shared container and health check (#25)

Replace per-test container startup (13 containers) with a single shared
container via TestMain, using per-test key prefixes for isolation.
Add a health check in TestMain to ensure etcd is ready before tests run.

Reduces test runtime from ~39s to ~6s and eliminates timeout flakiness
caused by Docker resource contention during parallel test execution.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: broken contact links (#23) + motion polish & count-up animation (#24) (#25)

#23 — All inline page CTAs and sidebar links now use `import.meta.env.BASE_URL`
so they resolve correctly on GitHub Pages from any subpath (avoids /services/contact etc).

#24 — Hero entrance slowed to 0.80s (was 0.65s), scroll-reveal to 0.70s (was 0.55s),
stagger delays stretched proportionally. Count-up animation added for .proof__num and
.bio-stat__num elements via IntersectionObserver (animate-once, easeOutCubic, 1.4s).

commit.message:
fix: backend persistence + cross-device sync (#106)

* fix: backend persistence + cross-device sync for all pages

- Fix _db_populate_profiles user_id bug: read from Report row instead
  of user_profile.get("id") which was always None. All 5 profile layer
  tables now populate after report generation.

- Add useIntake() hook with DB fallback: tries sessionStorage first,
  then fetches from server profile. Enables cross-device sync.

- Update all 6 pages (intelligence, healing, creative, perspective,
  wealth, dashboard) to use useIntake() instead of getStoredIntake().

- Fix cross-system synthesis: POST /integration/synthesize now reads
  profile data from DB when request fields are empty.

- Fix ephemeral activity tracking: POST /outcomes/activity now persists
  to outcome_metrics table. GET /outcomes/summary hydrates from DB.

commit.message:
Update docs and README for category system (#27)

* Add action figure category support with sidebar switcher

Introduces a category system (Transformers / Action Figures) so collectors
of non-transforming figures (GI Joe, Star Wars, He-Man, etc.) get their
own filtered experience. Sidebar toggle switches between categories;
all views, leaderboards, market pulse, and stats filter by active category.

- DB migration adds category column to Figures table
- Category switcher in sidebar with collapsed abbreviations (TF/AF)
- Scale-based class tiers for action figures (3.75", 6", 7", 12")
- Opt-in "Has Transformation?" checkbox on submission/scorecard forms
  (default checked for Transformers, unchecked for Action Figures)
- Dynamic score formula: /90 with transformation, /70 without
- All 16 API endpoints filter by ?category= query param
- All frontend fetches pass active category

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Bump styles.css version to bust cache for category switcher

The category switcher CSS wasn't loading because styles.css cache
version wasn't bumped with the other files.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix category switcher crushed by flex shrink in sidebar

The sidebar is a flex column and when content exceeded viewport height,
the category-switcher was shrinking to 1.8px due to default flex-shrink.
Adding flex-shrink: 0 prevents it from collapsing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Filter brand dropdown by active category

When in Action Figures mode, brand dropdown only shows brands that
have existing action figures (empty for new category). Transformer
approved brands only appear in Transformer mode. Users can always
add new brands via "Other" option.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Update documentation and README for category system

- Add new "Categories" section (02) to in-app docs explaining the
  Transformers/Action Figures switcher, size tiers, and transformation opt-in
- Update Platform Overview to mention both categories
- Update Trade Scan section: physical quality now shows 7 core + 2 optional
  transformation attributes
- Update Grading System: dynamic approval score formula (/90 vs /70)
- Renumber all doc sections (02-17 → 03-18)
- Add Categories & Scoring section to README.md

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: add web dashboard, admin portal, S3 report storage, and repo cleanup

Web Dashboard (web/)
- Flask app with client onboarding portal (/)
- Admin dashboard (/admin) with session-based login, protected by ADMIN_USER/ADMIN_PASSWORD env vars
- Admin login page (/admin/login) with violet-themed UI
- Dual report generation per scan: teaser (client-facing) + full (admin-only)
- Both reports uploaded to S3 — reports/{client}/{date}/{job_id}[-full].html
- S3 report listing grouped by client with links to both report types
- Log out button, Admin nav link on client portal
- All 32 AWS regions in the region dropdown, grouped by geography
- Auto-loads .env via python-dotenv on startup

CloudFormation
- Added default TrustedAccountId (919729584426) to optivulnix-role.yaml

Dependencies
- Pin flask==3.1.3 and python-dotenv==1.2.2 in requirements.txt + requirements-dev.txt
- Add "web" extras_require in setup.py

Repo cleanup
- Remove setup-github.sh (one-time use script)
- Fix .gitignore: replace broad *.html rule with specific patterns (was blocking web/templates)
- Add web/reports/ and demo-report* to .gitignore
- Add make web target; make clean now also wipes web/reports/

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add episodic memory — Archimedes learns from every chat interaction

EventMemory.cs (new):
- SQLite table storing every chat exchange: message, command, reply, output, success
- Recall(): keyword-based relevance search → returns top-N past events for a query
- FormatForPrompt(): formats memories as MEMORY: block injected into system prompt
- GetFailures(): exposes recurring failures to SelfImprovementEngine for learning
- Auto-prunes to last 200 events to stay lean

Program.cs:
- eventMemory initialized at startup
- /chat/stream: recall relevant past events BEFORE LLM call → inject into system prompt
- /chat/stream: save every exchange AFTER response → fire-and-forget Task.Run
- GET /memory: inspect what Archimedes has learned (stats + recent + failures)
- SelfImprovementEngine.SetEventMemory() called at startup

SelfImprovementEngine.cs:
- SetEventMemory() / GetRecentFailures() / GetMemoryStats() added
- Engine can now analyze chat failure patterns as input for self-improvement

Result: Archimedes remembers past interactions. "yes" to a question now
has full context. Recurring failures surface for self-analysis.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add version bump script and update release workflow (#80)

## What does this PR do?

This PR adds a new `bump_version.py` script that automates version
updates across all project files and updates the release workflow
documentation.

### Changes:

1. **New `scripts/bump_version.py`**: A utility script that:
   - Validates new versions are in valid semver format (X.Y.Z)
   - Ensures version bumps are valid (patch, minor, or major only)
   - Updates version numbers in all relevant files:
     - `server.json` (top-level and packages version)
     - `.claude-plugin/plugin.json`
     - `.claude-plugin/marketplace.json`
     - `.mcp.json` (pinned uvx version in args)
   - Provides clear error messages for invalid bumps

2. **Updated `pyproject.toml`**: Added `bump-version` poe task that runs
the script

3. **Updated `.mcp.json`**: Pinned the `mcp-outline` uvx version to
`1.7.1` (required for the bump script to work correctly)

4. **Updated `CLAUDE.md`**: Expanded version tagging section with:
   - Clear version numbering rules
   - Instructions for using the bump script
   - Complete release workflow steps

The script prevents common versioning mistakes by validating semver
compliance and rejecting invalid bumps (downgrades, arbitrary jumps,
etc.).

## Checklist

- [ ] I ran `uv run pre-commit install` and committed with hooks enabled
- [ ] Tests pass locally (`uv run pytest tests/ -v`)

https://claude.ai/code/session_016kEZ77yr8QyGGUvbwGaqMV

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix hippocampus gradient flow: gate and pattern_sep now learn

Previously .item() on alpha and torch.no_grad() on encode_key killed
all gradient to the reinstatement gate and pattern separation network.

Now:
- alpha stays as tensor through PFC blend (grad → gate weights)
- sim_t computed differentiably via torch dot product (grad → pattern_sep)
- gate_input uses sim_t tensor, not float (grad flows through similarity)
- Numpy similarity still used for fast top-k selection (no grad needed)
- .item() only for non-differentiable ops (strength updates, comparisons)

Gradient path: CE/critic loss → PFC state → alpha_t → gate → gate weights
                                           → sim_t → key → pattern_sep weights

commit.message:
On-device hidden state: chain norm+matmul and residual+FFN traces (~235ms/tok)

- Keep hidden state on chip 0 device throughout all 32 layers
- Chain RMSNorm + matmul into per-layer traces (eliminates norm_out PCIe writes)
- Chain residual_add + RMSNorm + FFN + residual_add into per-layer traces
  (eliminates FFN PCIe write+read round-trips entirely)
- FFN compute now runs asynchronously, hidden in next layer's blocking read
- Output projections still on chip 1 via device_gemv
- Saves ~20ms/tok (255→235ms) by eliminating 64 PCIe transfers

feat: ui overhaul — ACL button status, dataset table cleanup (#7)

- Remove redundant "Full Path" column from dataset table (tree
  indentation already makes the hierarchy clear)
- Add GET /api/acl-status endpoint: returns map[dataset]bool by
  running getfacl per mounted filesystem; falls back to acltype
  ZFS property when getfacl/nfs4_getfacl are unavailable
- ACL button in dataset table now lights up (active class + CSS)
  when the dataset has non-trivial ACL entries configured
- acl_set_posix.yml / acl_set_nfs4.yml now set acltype=posixacl /
  nfsv4acls before calling setfacl, keeping the ZFS property in
  sync with actual ACL state
- Decouple /api/acl-status fetch from main Promise.all so a failure
  does not break the initial page load
- Add .btn-acl.active CSS rule (was missing; NFS button had it)
- refreshACLStatus() called after every ACL mutation and on load
